calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-04-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexander Biddle, via ZOOM, counsel for Gail Yoshida, Executor.

At the outset, the court queries counsel if he has any additions to his written responses. Counsel has none. The court queries counsel as to the $2,501.05 paid for a funeral dinner. Counsel reports that the total was to feed thirty-four people and believes that it was not an unreasonable amount to pay for a funeral meal. The court then points to the request for extraordinary fees which will require timesheets. Counsel reports that he was unaware of the court's timekeeping rules. The court then advises of the issues regarding the request for payment for administrative support provided by Swift Attorney Services which are not recoverable. Counsel reports that was a misunderstanding on his part and believes it is fair for the court to deny the request for those fees.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Petition is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition Pursuant To Court Order; To Determine Heirs And To Request An Order To Show Cause Hearing Regarding Validity Of Decedents Will
Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Continued To Apr-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexander Biddle for and with Gail Yoshida (Executor).

The Court stated that there now seems to be a number of potential Beneficiaries/Heirs who are required to receive notice and questions why no proof, or supporting documents as to who the Heirs are was provided. Mr. Biddle stated that notice was sent, that a proof of service was filed yesterday (copy provided) and that all of the known Beneficiaries received timely notice. Mr. Biddle stated that he can ask the Investigator for supporting documents, but the information will be in Japanese. The Court stated that it needs to be translated and that it should be done by a Certified Translator. Mr. Biddle stated that the additional Beneficiaries/Heirs are not provided for under the will, that he is not treating them as Heirs, that they don't provide family records for all probate matters that they file and requested three months to get the information from the Investigator. The Petition to Determine Heirs, etc.is continued to April 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-19-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexander Biddle for and with Gail Yoshida (Petitioner).

The Court stated that the issue is whether there was adequate diligence in finding potential heirs, and that it understands the difficulty given the number of potential heirs and where they are located. Ms. Yoshida stated that each city has its own registry for the family living in that city, that when a daughter marries she leaves her family registry and joins her husband's registry, spoke of Masae's life history, and stated that Masae was cut off from her family. Ms. Yoshida also stated that Masae only returned to Japan once when her GI husband brought her back and that her only contact was with her younger sister. Mr. Biddle stated that the difficulty they are having is that they would have to go through every single registry in the Country. The Court stated that it is inclined to issue letters and set status on close of estate, but before the estate closes Counsel will have to bring a petition to determine heirship which may have/require a higher level of diligence done in locating heirs. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to receipt and review of the order). Bond is waived. Status on close of estate is set for June 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Counsel that close of the estate will not be considered unless a petition to determine heirship is adjudicated.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Not reported.
